channel.title: Performance-Management on Abdulkadir Güngör
channel.description: Recent content in Performance-Management on Abdulkadir Güngör

item.title: Change Tracking and Performance in EF Core: State Management and AsNoTracking Scenarios
item.description: Entity Framework Core (EF Core) employs a highly complex mechanism behind the scenes while managing database operations with an object-oriented approach. The heart of this mechanism is the Change Tracker unit. The scalability and response time of an enterprise application are directly dependent on how efficiently this unit is utilized.

item.title: CQRS: Architecturally Separating Write and Read Operations
item.description: As scalability and performance requirements increase in modern software architectures, traditional data access models (CRUD) have begun to fall short. CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) aims to overcome these bottlenecks by completely separating the data update (Command) and data read (Query) operations in a system. Popularized by Greg Young, this pattern is based on Bertrand Meyer’s CQS (Command-Query Separation) principle.
